(born October 11, 1983) is a retired [Canadian](/wiki/Canadians "Canadians") professional [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts"). A [World Victory Road](/wiki/World_Victory_Road "World Victory Road"), [Aggression Fighting Championship](/wiki/Aggression_Fighting_Championship "Aggression Fighting Championship"), [King of the Cage](/wiki/King_of_the_Cage "King of the Cage") and [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") veteran, Denis is best known for being a former KOTC Canada Bantamweight Champion, semi-finalist in the Sengoku Featherweight Tournament and his Knockout of the Night victory in his debut fight in the UFC.

Canadian mixed martial arts fighter

**Nick Denis** (born October 11, 1983) is a retired [Canadian](/wiki/Canadians "Canadians") professional [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts"). A [World Victory Road](/wiki/World_Victory_Road "World Victory Road"), [Aggression Fighting Championship](/wiki/Aggression_Fighting_Championship "Aggression Fighting Championship"), [King of the Cage](/wiki/King_of_the_Cage "King of the Cage") and [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") veteran, Denis is best known for being a former KOTC Canada Bantamweight Champion, semi-finalist in the Sengoku Featherweight Tournament and his Knockout of the Night victory in his debut fight in the UFC.

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nick_Denis&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nick_Denis&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early career")]

Denis made his MMA debut in his native Canada in October 2006. He debuted for the [King of the Cage Canada](/wiki/King_of_the_Cage "King of the Cage") promotion and quickly became their Bantamweight champion.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

In 2009, Denis made his international MMA debut for the [World Victory Road](/wiki/World_Victory_Road "World Victory Road") promotion. He fought in the Sengoku Featherweight Grandprix, making it to the semifinal round.

More recently, he fought against [Bellator](/wiki/Bellator_Fighting_Championships "Bellator Fighting Championships") veteran [Nick Mamalis](/wiki/Nick_Mamalis "Nick Mamalis") who he defeated via KO (suplex and chokeslam) in the second round.

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nick_Denis&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

In early December 2011, it was announced that Denis had signed a multi-fight deal with the UFC.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In his UFC debut, Denis faced Joseph Sandoval on January 20, 2012 at UFC on FX: Guillard vs. Miller. He was successful, defeating Sandoval with a series of standing elbows.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Nick was expected to face [Johnny Bedford](/wiki/Johnny_Bedford "Johnny Bedford") at [UFC on Fox 3](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_3 "UFC on Fox 3") on May 5, 2012.[[6]](#cite_note-6) However, Bedford was forced out of the bout with an injury and replaced by [Roland Delorme](/wiki/Roland_Delorme "Roland Delorme").[[7]](#cite_note-BedfordOut/DelormeIn-7) He lost to Delorme by rear-naked choke at 4:59 of an action-packed first round where both fighters were rocked until Delorme rallied and submitted Denis.

In November 2012, Nick announced his retirement from the sport of MMA in a post on his blog, citing research of [Chronic traumatic encephalopathy](/wiki/Chronic_traumatic_encephalopathy "Chronic traumatic encephalopathy").[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9) Denis is frequently quoted and his research is the subject of many discussions on media outlets including [MMA Live](/wiki/MMA_Live "MMA Live"), [The New York Daily News](/wiki/The_New_York_Daily_News "The New York Daily News") and [The Joe Rogan Experience](/wiki/The_Joe_Rogan_Experience "The Joe Rogan Experience").

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nick_Denis&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Denis studied at the [University of Ottawa](/wiki/University_of_Ottawa "University of Ottawa"), earning a [Bachelor of Science](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Science "Bachelor of Science") in [Biochemistry](/wiki/Biochemistry "Biochemistry"). Nick also invested part of his "Knockout of the Night" win bonus in purchasing land, owning a large part of land in [North Stormont, Ontario](/wiki/North_Stormont,_Ontario "North Stormont, Ontario"), Canada. A fan of board games, Denis has recently designed an algorithm for efficiently playing *[SeaFall](/wiki/SeaFall "SeaFall")*.

## Championships and accomplishments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nick_Denis&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Championships and accomplishments")]

* **[King of the Cage](/wiki/King_of_the_Cage "King of the Cage")**
  + [KOTC Canada Bantamweight Championship](/wiki/List_of_KOTC_champions "List of KOTC champions") (One time)
  + One successful title defense
* **[Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship")**
  + [Knockout of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") (One time) vs. Joseph Sandoval
